question: Develop a C++ program that prompts the user to enter their first name (as a string), age (as an integer), and height in meters (as a double). After receiving all inputs, print a formatted sentence summarizing this information (e.g., 'Hello [Name], you are [Age] years old and [Height] meters tall.').

test_cases.expected: Hello Bob, you are 40 years old and 1.83 meters tall.

errors: The 'height' variable is declared as an 'int' instead of a 'double'. Although the input prompt asks for height in meters (which can be a decimal), reading a double value (e.g., 1.83) into an integer variable will cause truncation (e.g., 1), losing the fractional part and leading to semantically incorrect output for height.
